statementLines: Consider the following statements: With reference to the Constitution of India, if an area in a State is declared as Scheduled Area under the Fifth Schedule-
I. the State Government loses its executive power in such areas and the local body assumes total administration.
II. the Union Government can take over the total administration of such areas under certain circumstances on the recommendations of the Governor.
Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
questions.questionText: Consider the following statements: With reference to the Constitution of India, if an area in a State is declared as Scheduled Area under the Fifth Schedule- I. the State Government loses its executive power in such areas and the local body assumes total administration. II. the Union Government can take over the total administration of such areas under certain circumstances on the recommendations of the Governor. Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
questions.explanationMarkdown:
**Explanation:** Option d is the correct answer. **The provisions of the Fifth Schedule of the Constitution of India apply to the administration and control of the Scheduled Areas and Scheduled Tribes in any State other than the States of Assam, Meghalaya, Tripura and Mizoram.**

Statement I is incorrect.**The executive power of a State extends to the Scheduled Areas, subject to the other **provisions of the Fifth Schedule**. Also, the Governor of the state has a special responsibility regarding such Scheduled Areas of the concerned state. He has to submit a report to the President regarding the administration of such areas, annually or whenever so required by the President. The executive power of the Centre extends to giving directions to the States regarding the administration of such areas. Each state having scheduled areas has to establish a tribes advisory council to advise on welfare and advancement of the scheduled tribes.**

Statement II is incorrect. **There is**no provision under the Fifth Schedule of the Constitution of India that allows **the Union Government to take over the total administration of Scheduled Areas.** Instead, the Governor is empowered to direct that any particular act of Parliament or the state legislature does not apply to a scheduled area or apply with specified modifications and exceptions.

statementLines: With reference to Panchayats at village level (Gram Panchayats) in India, consider the following statements:
I. All the seats in a Panchayat at village level shall be filled by persons chosen by direct election from territorial constituencies in the Panchayat area.
II. As per the 73rd Constitutional Amendment Act, 1992 the Chairperson of the Panchayat at village level shall be elected directly by the Gram Sabha.
III. It is the executive body of the Gram Sabha, responsible to the Gram Sabha.
Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
questions.questionText: With reference to Panchayats at village level (Gram Panchayats) in India, consider the following statements: I. All the seats in a Panchayat at village level shall be filled by persons chosen by direct election from territorial constituencies in the Panchayat area. II. As per the 73rd Constitutional Amendment Act, 1992 the Chairperson of the Panchayat at village level shall be elected directly by the Gram Sabha. III. It is the executive body of the Gram Sabha, responsible to the Gram Sabha. Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
questions.explanationMarkdown:
**Explanation:** Option a is the correct answer.**Gram Panchayats form the lowest tier of India's three-tier Panchayati Raj system established under the**73rd **Constitutional Amendment Act, 1992**.**

Statement I is correct.**As per Article 243C(2) of the Constitution of India, All the seats in a Panchayat (including a **Panchayat at village level) shall be filled by persons chosen by direct election from territorial constituencies in the **Panchayat area **and, for this purpose, each Panchayat area shall be divided into territorial constituencies in such manner that the ratio between the population of each constituency and the number of seats allotted to it shall, so far as practicable, be the same throughout the Panchayat area. Here, "Panchayat area" means the territorial area of a Panchayat (in this case the Panchayat at village level). Also, the "Gram Sabha" means a body consisting of persons registered in the electoral rolls relating to a village comprised within the area of Panchayat at the village level. So, ultimately voters from Gram Sabha choose the persons to fill the seats by direct elections in Panchayat at village level.**

Statement II is incorrect.**The 73rd Constitutional Amendment Act, 1992 does not mandate whether the Chairperson of the Panchayat at village level should be elected directly by the people or indirectly by the elected Panchayat members.**Rather, the amendment states that the Chairperson of a Panchayat at the village level shall be elected in **such manner as the Legislature of a State may, by law, provide. **

Statement III is correct.**The Panchayat at the village level (Gram Panchayat) is the**executive body of the Gram **Sabha**. Its members are responsible for the day-to-day administration of the village and**are responsible to the Gram **Sabha of the village.** The Panchayat at the village level handles developmental works like sanitation, maintenance works, rural electrification, and programmes related to women's empowerment, child welfare, etc.

I. Maintenance of financial accounts of the Gram Panchayat
II. Maintenance of land records of the village/s

questions.questionText: Patwari, known with different names and an important functionary at the Gram Panchayat level in several states in India, is responsible for which of the following functions? I. Maintenance of financial accounts of the Gram Panchayat II. Maintenance of land records of the village/s III. Maintenance of official records of the proceedings of the Gram Sabha. IV. Collection of land revenue from the farmers Select the correct answer using the code given below:
questions.explanationMarkdown:
**Explanation:** Option b is the correct answer.**At the village level, the**Patwari **is an important revenue functionary.**

Statement I is incorrect.**Maintaining financial accounts, expenditure details, and administrative records of the Gram Panchayat is the responsibility of the**Panchayat Secretary.**

Statement II is correct.**The major duty of the Patwari is to**maintain and update village land records**, including maps, ownership details, field measurements, and changes in land use.**

Statement III is incorrect.**Proceedings of the Gram Sabha**are maintained by the**Panchayat Secretary **and not by the Patwari.**

Statement IV is correct.**One of the Patwaris' key responsibilities is to**organise the collection of land revenue from **the farmers**. The Patwari also reports crop patterns and agricultural changes to the government.

questions.questionText: With reference to the Panchayati Raj system in India, the members of Gram Sabha consists of:

options.a: All the persons residing in the village who are above 18 years of age.
options.b: All the persons who are permanent residents of the village.
options.c: All persons of the village who are registered as voters in the electoral roll of that village.
options.d: All the persons who own any kind of immovable property in the villag

questions.explanationMarkdown: **Explanation:** Option c is the correct answer.**The**Gram Sabha**is the cornerstone of the Panchayati Raj system. It ensures direct participation of people in decision-making and monitoring of development activities. Under** Article 243 **of the Constitution of India, the** "Gram Sabha" **means a body consisting of**persons registered in **the**electoral rolls**of the village comprising the area of the Gram Panchayat.

statementLines: Which of the following statements about the National Centre for Good Governance (NCGG) are correct?
1. NCGG was set up by the Sardar Vallabhbhai Patel National Police Academy.
2. The National Institute of Administrative Research (NIAR) has been rechristened and subsumed into NCGG.
3. NCGG is an apex-level autonomous body under the Ministry of Home Affairs.
4. The Chairperson of the Governing Body of the NCGG is the Cabinet Secretary to the Government of India.
Select the correct answer using the code given below:
questions.questionText: Which of the following statements about the National Centre for Good Governance (NCGG) are correct? 1. NCGG was set up by the Sardar Vallabhbhai Patel National Police Academy. 2. The National Institute of Administrative Research (NIAR) has been rechristened and subsumed into NCGG. 3. NCGG is an apex-level autonomous body under the Ministry of Home Affairs. 4. The Chairperson of the Governing Body of the NCGG is the Cabinet Secretary to the Government of India. Select the correct answer using the code given below:
questions.explanationMarkdown:
**Explanation:** Option b is the correct answer **

Statement 1 is incorrect: **The**National Centre for Good Governance **(NCGG) was**not established by the Sardar **Vallabhbhai Patel National Police Academy. **Instead, it was**set up by the Government of India in 2014 **under the Department of Administrative Reforms and Public Grievances (DARPG). The NCGG deals with a gamut of governance issues from local, state to national levels, across all sectors focus, and also on policy reform, capacity building and training of civil servants of India and other developing countries.**

Statement 2 is correct: **The**National Institute of Administrative Research **(NIAR), which was** established in 1995 **by the Lal Bahadur Shastri National Academy of Administration (LBSNAA), has indeed been**rechristened and subsumed **into NCGG**. This helped consolidate training and research resources for administrative capacity building under a single institution.**

Statement 3 is incorrect: NCGG **is an**apex-level autonomous institution, **but it is**under th**e DARPG, which operates under the**Ministry of Personnel, Public Grievances, and Pensions-not the Ministry of Home Affairs. **

Statement 4 is correct: **The**Cabinet Secretary**, who is the highest-ranking civil servant in the Government of India, serves as the**Chairperson of NCGG's Governing Body. **

statementLines: Consider the following statements regarding the Central Council of Local Government:
I. It was established under the provisions of Article 263 of the Indian Constitution through a notification issued by the President of India.
II. Presently, it deals with matters relating to Urban Local Government only.
III. The council is headed by the Prime Minister of India.
Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
questions.questionText: Consider the following statements regarding the Central Council of Local Government: I. It was established under the provisions of Article 263 of the Indian Constitution through a notification issued by the President of India. II. Presently, it deals with matters relating to Urban Local Government only. III. The council is headed by the Prime Minister of India. Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
questions.explanationMarkdown:
**Explanation:** Option c is the correct answer.**

Statement I is correct: The Central Council of Local Government**was**set up in 1954**. It was constituted**under **Article 263**of the Constitution of India**by an order of the President of India**. The provision of Article 263 of the Constitution was invoked for the first time on 9 August 1952 when, by a notification, the Central Council of Health was establish. By similar notifications the President established the Central Council for Local Government and Urban Development on 6 September 1954**

Statement II is correct: **Originally, it was known as the Central Council of Local Self-Government. However, the term 'self-government' was found to be superfluous and hence was replaced by the term 'government' in the 1980s**. Till **1958, it dealt with both urban as well as rural local governments, but after 1958 it dealt with matters of urban local **government only.**

Statement III is incorrect: **The Council is an**advisory body**. It**consists of the Minister for Urban Development **(Ministry of Housing and Urban Affairs) **in the Government of India and the ministers for local self-government in states. The**Union Minister for Urban Development (Ministry of Housing and Urban Affairs) acts as the Chairman of **the Council**.

questions.questionText: With reference to the Indian polity, which of the following statements best describes the Principle of Subsidiarity? A principle which states that- delegated to the lower levels. them out.

options.a: All tasks should be performed by higher levels of government to ensure uniform decisions across the country.
options.b: Core functions of government should be performed at higher levels and less important functions should be
options.c: Functions of government should be assigned to the lowest possible level of government that can effectively carry
options.d: Lower levels of government should act only when higher levels of government cannot manage a task on their own.

questions.explanationMarkdown:
**Explanation:** Option c is the correct answer.**The**Principle of Subsidiarity**emphasises taking decisions**as close to the people as possible**. It states that a**larger or **higher level of government should not perform a task that can be effectively carried out by a smaller or local level **of authority**. Governance functions must be assigned to the**lowest possible level of governance that can effectively **carry them out **and only when that level is unable to perform them should responsibilities move upward from village level to local bodies, then to the State, and finally to the Union government. This principle promotes efficiency, strengthens democratic participation, and ensures clarity in the allocation of responsibilities.
For example in the case of Panchayati Raj system in India, the 'Principle of Subsidiarity', means that the panchayats at the higher level should not assume the powers and authority of any panchayats at the lower level or of the Gram Sabha.

questions.explanationMarkdown: **Explanation:** Option c is the correct answer. ** Option I is correct: In 1989, P.K. Thungon Committee recommended constitutional recognition**for**the local **government bodies.**A constitutional amendment to provide for periodic elections to local government institutions, and enlistment of appropriate functions to the local governments, along with funds, was also recommended.  Option II is correct: **The L.M. Singhvi Committee, formally known as the Committee on Revitalisation of Panchayati Raj Institutions for Democracy and Development, was constituted by the Rajiv Gandhi government in 1986.** It **recommended that Panchayati Raj Institutions be granted constitutional recognition to strengthen their stability, **legitimacy, and authority within India's governance system. ** Option III is correct: In 1977, Ashok Mehta Committee made several recommendations for making panchayats **institutions strong. This committee also prepared a draft to amend the constitution to reconstruct, reinforce and **revitalize the Panchayati Raj Institutions to make them an organic part of the national democratic process and **elevate the panchayats. **

statementLines: Consider the following:
I. Addition of Twelfth Schedule to the Constitution
II. Addition of Part IX-B to the Constitution
III. Amendment to Article 40 under Part IV of the Constitution

IV. Addition of Provisions related to the constitution of the State Finance Commission in the Constitution Which of the above changes were made to the Constitution of India through the Constitution (Seventy Fourth Amendment) Act, 1992?
questions.questionText: Consider the following: I. Addition of Twelfth Schedule to the Constitution II. Addition of Part IX-B to the Constitution III. Amendment to Article 40 under Part IV of the Constitution IV. Addition of Provisions related to the constitution of the State Finance Commission in the Constitution Which of the above changes were made to the Constitution of India through the Constitution (Seventy Fourth Amendment) Act, 1992?
questions.explanationMarkdown: **Explanation:** Option a is the correct answer. **The Constitution (74th Amendment) Act, 1992, which came into force on June 1, 1993, set up Urban Local Bodies (ULBs). It granted constitutional status to municipalities.** Option I is correct: **The Constitution (74th Amendment) Act, 1992,** inserted the 12th Schedule **listing 18 municipal functions for the Urban local bodies.** Option II is incorrect: It added a new Part IX-A (not IX-B) **to the Constitution, covering Articles 243P to 243ZG, specifically related to municipalities.** Option III is incorrect: Article 40**is part of the Directive Principles of State Policy (DPSP) and directs the State to organise village panchayats and endow them with the necessary powers and authority to function as units of self-government.**It has not been amended as part of the**Constitution (74th Amendment) Act,**1992. ** Option IV is incorrect: The Constitution of India provides for the establishment of the State Finance Commission **under Article 243 I (not under Article 243 Y) by the State Government **to review the financial position of Panchayats. Under Article 243Y of the Constitution of India, it provides that the Finance Commission constituted under Article 243-I shall also review the financial position of the Municipalities and make recommendations to the Governor.

statementLines: Consider the following states of India:
I. Nagaland
II. Meghalaya
III. Mizoram In
how many of the above given states are the provisions of Part IX of the Constitution of India not applicable currently?
questions.questionText: Consider the following states of India: I. Nagaland II. Meghalaya III. Mizoram In how many of the above given states are the provisions of Part IX of the Constitution of India not applicable currently?
questions.explanationMarkdown: **Explanation:** Option c is the correct answer. **According to**Article 243M of the Constitution**, the provisions of**Part IX dealing with Panchayats**do not apply to the Scheduled Areas under Article 244(1) and the Tribal Areas under Article 244(2), and are also exempted from application in the States of**Nagaland, Meghalaya and Mizoram**, as well as in the hill areas of Manipur that have functioning District Councils. Additionally, the Panchayats at the district level are not applicable to the hill areas of the Darjeeling district in West Bengal due to the existence of the Darjeeling Gorkha Hill Council, and nothing in Part IX shall reduce or alter its powers. Article 243D, relating to reservation of seats for Scheduled Castes in Panchayats, is also not applicable to Arunachal Pradesh.**However, the Constitution allows flexibility to the State Legislatures of Nagaland, Meghalaya and Mizoram may **extend the provisions of Part IX to their States through a resolution (passed by a majority of the total membership **of that House and by a majority of not less than two-thirds of the members of that House present and voting), **and Parliament may extend Part IX to the Scheduled and Tribal Areas with suitable modifications without treating it as a constitutional amendment under Article 368 of the Constitution of India.

statementLines: Under which Schedule of the Constitution of India can the transfer of tribal land to private parties for mining be declared null and void?
questions.questionText: Under which Schedule of the Constitution of India can the transfer of tribal land to private parties for mining be declared null and void?
questions.explanationMarkdown:
**Explanation:** Option b is the correct answer.**The Fifth Schedule contains provisions for administration of schedule and tribal areas. **Under the Fifth Schedule of the Constitution of India, the governor of a state can make regulations that prohibits or restricts transfer of land by Schedule Tribes.**In Samatha vs. State of Andhra Pradesh (1997), the Supreme Court declared that the transfer of tribal land to **private parties for mining was null and void under the Fifth Schedule. **The scheduled areas are treated differently from the other areas in the country because they are inhabited by 'aboriginals' who are socially and economically rather backward, and special efforts need to be made to improve their condition.**

Option a is incorrect**. Third Schedule**contains provisions for Oath and affirmation of Constitutional offices like Ministers, Member of Parliament, Supreme court etc.
**Option c is incorrect**. Ninth Schedule **contains provisions that legislation which aims at implementing land reforms in the country shall be exempted from Judicial review. Under I.R. Coelho case, the supreme court brought the Ninth Schedule in the domain of Judicial review.**

Option d is incorrect**. The Twelfth Schedule**contains a list of subjects for administration by Municipalities (local bodies).

statementLines: The Constitution (Seventy-Third Amendment) Act, 1992 and The Constitution (Seventy-Fourth Amendment) Act, 1992 came into force during the tenure of which of the following Prime Ministers of India?
questions.questionText: The Constitution (Seventy-Third Amendment) Act, 1992 and The Constitution (Seventy-Fourth Amendment) Act, 1992 came into force during the tenure of which of the following Prime Ministers of India?
questions.explanationMarkdown: **Explanation:** Option a is the correct answer. **From 1947 to 1993, the local governance system underwent several policy experiments, yet lacked uniformity in structure and empowerment. After decades of deliberation, the Central Government introduced two constitutional amendment proposals in 1989 to strengthen and standardise local governments across India. This process resulted in 1992, when the Parliament passed the 73rd Constitutional Amendment Act, providing constitutional status to Panchayati Raj Institutions (PRIs), and the 74th Constitutional Amendment Act, doing the same for Urban Local Bodies (Nagarpalikas).**Both amendments came into force in April 1993, revitalising grassroots **democracy during the tenure of Prime Minister P. V. Narasimha Rao (**1991-19**96).**

Statement II: The Constitution of India provides that the Panchayats at the intermediate level may not be constituted in a State having a population not exceeding twenty lakhs..
Which one of the following is correct in respect of the above statements?
questions.questionText: With reference to local self-government in India, consider the following statements: Statement I: The states of Goa and Sikkim follow a Two-Tier Panchayati Raj System. Statement II: The Constitution of India provides that the Panchayats at the intermediate level may not be constituted in a State having a population not exceeding twenty lakhs.. Which one of the following is correct in respect of the above statements?

options.a: Both Statement I and Statement II are correct, and Statement II explains Statement I
options.b: Both Statement I and Statement II are correct and Statement II does not explain Statement I
options.c: Statement I is correct, but Statement II is not correct
options.d: Statement I is not correct, but Statement II is correct

questions.explanationMarkdown:
**Explanation:** Option a is the correct answer. **

Statement I is correct: In India, the two-tier Panchayat Raj system exists in the states /UTs of **Dadra & Nagar Haveli, Daman & Diu,**Goa, **Lakshadweep, Manipur, Puducherry and** Sikkim.**

Statement II is correct: **The constitution of India under Article 243 B provides for the establishment of the three-tier system with Panchayats at the village, intermediate, and district levels in every State however,**Article 243 B(2)**provides an exception that**States with populations below 20 lakh need not constitute Panchayats at the **intermediate (block) level**and they may have a**two-tier system**instead of three.**Hence,

option a is correct: **Both Statement I and Statement II are correct, and Statement II explains Statement I

statementLines: Consider the following statements regarding reservation of seats for women in Panchayat elections as per the Constitution of India:
I. Not less than one-third of the total number of seats to be filled by direct election in every Panchayat shall be reserved for women.
II. The reservation of seats for women does not extend to the offices of the Chairpersons in the Panchayats at the village or any other level.
Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
questions.questionText: Consider the following statements regarding reservation of seats for women in Panchayat elections as per the Constitution of India: I. Not less than one-third of the total number of seats to be filled by direct election in every Panchayat shall be reserved for women. II. The reservation of seats for women does not extend to the offices of the Chairpersons in the Panchayats at the village or any other level. Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
questions.explanationMarkdown:
**Explanation:** Option a is the correct answer**. The**73rd Constitutional Amendment Act of 1992**was a transformative step in decentralizing democracy to the grassroots level. One of its key contributions was empowering women through constitutionally mandated reservations under**Article 243D**in Panchayati Raj Institutions (PRIs).**

Statement I is correct.**Article**243D(3)**of the Constitution provides that**not less than one-third**of**the total number **of seats to be filled by direct election in every Panchayat shall be**reserved for women**(including the number of seats reserved for women belonging to the Scheduled Castes and the Scheduled Tribes).**

Statement II is incorrect.**As per the Article**243D(4)**of the Constitution of India**the offices of the Chairpersons in **the Panchayats at the village or any other level shall be reserved for**the Scheduled Castes, the Scheduled Tribes**and **women in such manner as the Legislature of a State may, by law, provide. **Provided that the number of offices of Chairpersons reserved for the Scheduled Castes and the Scheduled Tribes in the Panchayats at each level in any State shall bear, as nearly as may be, the same proportion to the total number of such offices in the Panchayats at each level as the population of the Scheduled Castes in the State or of the Scheduled Tribes in the State bears to the total population of the State.**Also the Article 243D(4) provided further that not less than one-third of the total number of offices of **Chairpersons in the Panchayats at each level shall be reserved for women: Provided also that the number of offices **reserved under this clause shall be allotted by rotation to different Panchayats at each level. **

source.sourceText: https://ncert.nic.in/textbook.php?keps2=8-10 - NCERT - Indian Constitution at Work - Local Governments - Reservations - Page 184 and 190; https://cdnbbsr.s3waas.gov.in/s380537a945c7aaa788ccfcdf1b99b5d8f/uploads/2024/07/20240716890312078.pdf (Article 243D, page 121)

statementLines: The fundamental object of the Panchayati Raj system is to ensure which among the following?
1. People's participation in development
2. Political accountability
3. Financial mobilization
Select the correct answer using the code given below.
questions.questionText: The fundamental object of the Panchayati Raj system is to ensure which among the following? 1. People's participation in development 2. Political accountability 3. Financial mobilization Select the correct answer using the code given below.
questions.explanationMarkdown:
**Explanation:** Option c is the correct answer.**Statements 1 is correct but 2 is incorrect. **Grass-roots democracy was established in India not to ensure any political accountability, but to ensure**citizen's participation in governance **and development leading to democratic decentralization. Moreover, political accountability already existed in the villages by way of elections to State legislatures.**

Statement 3 is incorrect**.**Financial mobilization was never the basis for the establishment of Panchayati raj. Local financial mobilization (by way of octroi taxes etc.) is the outcome of the establishment of the PRIs, not its objective. In fact, only a few states have devolved financial powers to their PRIs.

statementLines: The Panchayat (Extension to Scheduled Areas) Act, 1996 was enacted on the recommendations of which of the following committees?
questions.questionText: The Panchayat (Extension to Scheduled Areas) Act, 1996 was enacted on the recommendations of which of the following committees?
questions.explanationMarkdown:
**Explanation:** Option d is the correct answer**. The**Panchayat (Extension to Scheduled Areas) Act, 1996**, commonly known as**PESA Act**, was enacted to extend the provisions of Part IX of the Constitution of India to the**Scheduled Areas**under the Fifth Schedule of the Constitution. These areas are predominantly tribal and have unique socio-cultural practices.**

Option a is incorrect**.**Balwantrai Mehta Committee (1957)**was established to examine the Community Development Programme (1952) and the National Extension Service (1953) and suggest improvements.**Its Key Recommendations were:**Three-tier Panchayati Raj system**: Village (Gram Panchayat), Intermediate (Panchayat Samiti), and District (Zila Parishad). This committee laid the**foundation of Panchayati Raj in India**, inaugurated in Rajasthan in 1959.**

Option b is incorrect**.**Dinesh Goswami Committee (1990)**was concerned with**electoral reforms**.**Its Key Recommendations were:**Reducing money and muscle power in elections, Checking criminalisation of politics, Ensuring internal democracy in political parties.**

Option c is incorrect**.**Swaran Singh Committee (1976)**was appointed to suggest**constitutional amendments**for inclusion of**Fundamental Duties**. Its recommendations led to the addition of**Part IVA (Article 51A)**in the Constitution via the**42nd Constitutional Amendment Act, 1976**. It had**no relevance to Panchayati Raj**or Scheduled Areas governance.**

Option d is correct**.**The Narsimha Rao government appointed a committee headed by Mr. Dileep Singh Bhuria **(member of Parliament) in June 1994, to work out the details as to how structures similar to Panchayati Raj **Institutions can take shape in Tribal Areas and Scheduled Areas and to define their powers. **The Committee submitted its report in January 1995. The Bhuria Committee recommended a three-tier structure of self-governance in the tribal areas: (1) Gram Sabha-Every "habitation community" to have a Gram Sabha which will exercise command over natural resources, resolve disputes and manage institutions under it like schools and cooperatives;( 2) Gram Panchayat-Elected body of representatives of each Gram Sabha, also to function as an appellate authority for unresolved disputes at lower level; and (3) A block or taluka level body as the next higher level. Based on these recommendations, the**PESA Act was passed in 1996**, ensuring greater autonomy and recognition of tribal self-rule.

statementLines: With reference to the 73rd Constitutional Amendment to the Constitution of India, what major changes were introduced in the functioning of local governments?
I. Holding Panchayat elections at regular five-year intervals became a constitutional obligation.
II. The responsibility for conducting Panchayat elections was assigned to an independent State Election Commission.
III. It became mandatory for the States to assign the Panchayats the power to levy and collect various taxes and fees.
Select the correct answer using the code given below:
questions.questionText: With reference to the 73rd Constitutional Amendment to the Constitution of India, what major changes were introduced in the functioning of local governments? I. Holding Panchayat elections at regular five-year intervals became a constitutional obligation. II. The responsibility for conducting Panchayat elections was assigned to an independent State Election Commission. III. It became mandatory for the States to assign the Panchayats the power to levy and collect various taxes and fees. Select the correct answer using the code given below:
questions.explanationMarkdown:
**Explanation:** Option a is the correct answer. **The**73rd Constitutional Amendment Act, 1992**is a landmark reform that gave**constitutional status to the **Panchayati Raj Institutions**(PRIs). It**inserted Part IX (Articles 243 to 243-O)**into the Constitution and**added the **Eleventh Schedule**, listing**29 subjects**to be devolved to Panchayats. The amendment made the structure, powers, tenure, elections, and finances of Panchayats uniform and mandatory across all States (with specified exemptions). Before this amendment, local governments functioned only under State legislation, leading to irregular elections, unstable tenure, and weak financial arrangements.**

Statement I is correct:**Before the 73rd Amendment, states could delay or avoid holding Panchayat elections for years. After the amendment,**Article 243E mandates that every Panchayat shall continue for 5 years from the date **appointed for its first meeting.**Elections must be held before the expiry of its term, and even if the Panchayat is dissolved earlier, fresh elections must be conducted within six months of the date of dissolution. This provision ensures continuity, regularity, and stability in local self-government.**

Statement II is correct:**Before the amendment, Panchayat elections were conducted by state governments and were vulnerable to political interference. After the amendment,**Article 243K created an independent State Election Commission**(SEC), analogous to the Election Commission of India.**Article 243K(1)**provides that the "**superintendence, direction and control of the preparation of electoral rolls and the **conduct of all elections to the Panchayats shall be vested in the State Election Commission." **The**State Election Commission**is an independent constitutional authority,**ensuring free and fair Panchayat **elections. **

Statement III is incorrect**:**Article 243H empowers the Legislature of a State to authorise Panchayats to levy, **collect, and appropriate taxes, duties, fees, tolls**, etc., but**it is not compulsory**for States to assign such powers. It remains**discretionary** for the State Legislature.

statementLines: Consider the following key developments in the evolution of local self-government in pre-independence India:
I. Lord Ripon's Resolution on Local Self-Government
II. Lord Mayo's resolution for town based local bodies
III. Appointment of the Royal Commission on Decentralization
Which one of the following is the correct chronological order of above given events, starting from the earliest?
questions.questionText: Consider the following key developments in the evolution of local self-government in pre-independence India: I. Lord Ripon's Resolution on Local Self-Government II. Lord Mayo's resolution for town based local bodies III. Appointment of the Royal Commission on Decentralization Which one of the following is the correct chronological order of above given events, starting from the earliest?
questions.explanationMarkdown:
**Explanation:** Option c is the correct answer. **The sequence of above mentioned major developments in the evolution of local self-government in India is as **follows: **Option II: Lord Mayo's resolution for town based local bodies **commonly known as Lord Mayo's Resolution on Financial Decentralization (1870) was the first major step toward decentralization in British India. Mayo introduced the policy of financial decentralization, giving provinces greater responsibility for local expenditure. This laid the foundation for future local self-government reforms.**Option I:**Lord Ripon's Resolution on Local Self-Government (1882) **also Known as the**Magna Carta**of Local Self
- **Government in India.**1. Ripon's resolution emphasized elections, non-official majorities, and greater autonomy for local self-governments 2. This is considered the most important landmark in the evolution of municipal and rural local institutions. 3. Because of this, Ripon is known as the**"Father of Local Self-Government in India."**Option III: Appointment of the Royal Commission on Decentralization (1907), headed by Charles Hobhouse**, was appointed**to examine the functioning of local bodies**and recommend further devolution of powers. 1. It submitted its report in**1909 and recommended **that the village should be regarded as the basic unit of local self-government institutions, and every village should have a panchayat. Municipalities should be constituted in urban areas. Therefore, the** correct chronological order is: **1870 (Lord Mayo's Resolution for town based local bodies) **1882 (Lord Ripon's Resolution on Local Self Government)** 1907 (Appointment of the Royal Commission on Decentralization). **Hence, the** correct answer is: c) II-I-III **

statementLines: Consider the following statements in respect of the National Panchayati Raj Day:
Statement I: National Panchayati Raj Day is celebrated on 24th April every year in India.
Statement II: The 73rd Constitutional Amendment Act came into force on 24th April, 1993.
Which one of the following is correct in respect of the above statements?
questions.questionText: Consider the following statements in respect of the National Panchayati Raj Day: Statement I: National Panchayati Raj Day is celebrated on 24th April every year in India. Statement II: The 73rd Constitutional Amendment Act came into force on 24th April, 1993. Which one of the following is correct in respect of the above statements?

options.a: Both Statement I and Statement II are correct and Statement II explains Statement I
options.b: Both Statement I and Statement II are correct but Statement II does not explain Statement I
options.c: Statement I is correct but Statement II is not correct
options.d: Statement I is not correct but Statement II is correct

questions.explanationMarkdown:
**Explanation:** Option a is the correct answer. **

Statement I is correct:**The Panchayati Raj Day, recognized annually by the Ministry of Panchayati Raj on 24th **April**, commemorates the 73rd Amendment Act, 1992 of the Constitution coming into effect in the year 1993. This day celebrates the national local self-governance and democratic decentralization.**

Statement II is correct: **The**73rd Constitutional Amendment Act**(1992), which granted constitutional status to Panchayats and introduced Part IX of the Constitution,**came into force on 24th April 1993. **Why Statement II explains Statement I: **National Panchayati Raj Day is celebrated**specifically because**the 73rd Amendment became effective on**24 April **1993**. Thus, the reason for celebrating the day (Statement I) is directly explained by the fact given in Statement II. Hence,**Both Statement I and Statement II are correct and Statement II explains Statement I**.

statementLines: Consider the following statements regarding the dissolution of a Panchayat under the 73rd Constitutional Amendment Act, 1992:
I. When a Panchayat is dissolved and the remainder of its term is less than six months, it shall not be necessary to hold any election for constituting a Panchayat for such remainder term.
II. A Panchayat constituted upon the dissolution of a Panchayat shall continue only for the remainder of the period for which the dissolved Panchayat would have continued.
Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
questions.questionText: Consider the following statements regarding the dissolution of a Panchayat under the 73rd Constitutional Amendment Act, 1992: I. When a Panchayat is dissolved and the remainder of its term is less than six months, it shall not be necessary to hold any election for constituting a Panchayat for such remainder term. II. A Panchayat constituted upon the dissolution of a Panchayat shall continue only for the remainder of the period for which the dissolved Panchayat would have continued. Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
questions.explanationMarkdown:
**Explanation:** Option c is the correct answer. **The**73rd Constitutional Amendment Act, 1992**inserted Article 243E**into the Constitution, which**deals specifically **with the duration of Panchayats**and the situation arising when a Panchayat is dissolved before completing its normal tenure. Normal Tenure of a Panchayat Article 243E(1):**Every Panchayat has a five-year term**from the date of its first meeting. This tenure can end earlier only if the Panchayat is dissolved before the completion of five years. Once a Panchayat is dissolved, two important constitutional rules apply:**

Statement I is correct:**Article 243E(3)**explicitly provides that, if the remainder of the period for which the dissolved Panchayat would have continued is**less than six months**, it shall**not be necessary to hold any election**for constituting the Panchayat for such period. Hence, no election is required when the**unexpired term is less than six months**. This provision exists to prevent unnecessary expenditure and administrative complexity for a very short remaining period.**

Statement II is correct: Article 243E(4)**states that**where a Panchayat is dissolved and **an** election is required to be **held **(i.e., remainder term is six months or more), the n**ewly constituted Panchayat "shall continue only for the **remainder of the period for which the dissolved Panchayat would have continued**." Thus, the new Panchayat does not get a fresh five-year term; it only serves the unexpired portion of the dissolved Panchayat's term**. **

I. A person shall be disqualified for being chosen as, and for being, a member of a Panchayat if he/she is less than 25 years of age.
II. No election to any Municipality shall be called in question except by an election petition presented before the High Court of the concerned State.
Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
questions.questionText: With reference to the Constitution of India, consider the following statements: I. A person shall be disqualified for being chosen as, and for being, a member of a Panchayat if he/she is less than 25 years of age. II. No election to any Municipality shall be called in question except by an election petition presented before the High Court of the concerned State. Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
questions.explanationMarkdown:
**Explanation:** Option d is the correct answer.**

Statement I is incorrect: If a person is less than 25 years of age-Not a ground for disqualification **or being chosen as, and for being, a member of a Panchayat**. **Article 243F(1)a)**provides that a person shall be disqualified if "he/she is so disqualified by or under any law for the time being in force for the purposes of elections to the Legislature of the State concerned:." However, the article also provides that**no person shall be disqualified on the ground that he is less than twenty-five **years of age **for being a member of a Panchayat**, if he has attained the age of twenty-one years. **Therefore,**being below 25 years does not disqualify a person. **Being below 21 years would be a disqualification, but not 25.**

Statement II is incorrect.**Under**Article 243ZGb)**,**no election to any Municipality**shall be c**alled in question **except by an**election petition **presented to such**authority**and in such manner as is provided for by or under any** law made **by the Legislature of a State. **

statementLines: With reference to the Constitution of India, consider the following statements about a State Finance Commission (SFC):
I. It is constituted by the Governor of the State every five years.
II. It recommends grants-in-aid to Panchayats of the state to be given from the Consolidated Fund of India.

Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
questions.questionText: With reference to the Constitution of India, consider the following statements about a State Finance Commission (SFC): I. It is constituted by the Governor of the State every five years. II. It recommends grants-in-aid to Panchayats of the state to be given from the Consolidated Fund of India. III. The Governor of a state may, by order, provide qualifications which shall be requisite for appointment as members to the State Finance Commission. Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
questions.explanationMarkdown:
**Explanation:** Option a is the correct answer.**The**State Finance Commission (SFC)**is a constitutional body created**under Article 243-I**to strengthen fiscal decentralisation in India. Its primary mandate is to review the financial position of Panchayats and recommend how financial resources should be distributed between the State and local bodies.**

Statement I is correct.**The Constitution mandates that**the Governor shall constitute a State Finance Commission **every five years**to review the financial position of the Panchayats and to make recommendations to the Governor.**

Statement II is incorrect.**The SFC recommends**grants-in-aid to Panchayats from the Consolidated Fund of the **State**. Grants from the Consolidated Fund of India are recommended by the**Central Finance Commission under **Article 280.**

Statement III is incorrect.**As per Article 243I (2) of the Constitution of India,** the Legislature of a State may, by law, **provide (not the Governor of the state by order)**for the composition of the (State Finance) Commission,**the **qualifications which shall be requisite for appointment as members** thereof and the manner in which they shall be selected.

questions.questionText: With reference to the Constitution of India, consider the following statements about the State Election Commission (SEC): I. It is responsible for the superintendence, direction and control of the preparation of electoral rolls for the Panchayat elections. II. It is responsible for conducting elections to cooperative societies within the State. III. It decides matters related to the disqualification of members of Panchayats. Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
questions.explanationMarkdown:
**Explanation:** Option a is the correct answer.**The**State Election Commission (SEC)**is a constitutional authority established under**Article 243K**to ensure**free, fair, **and impartial elections to Panchayats and Municipalities**.**

Statement I is correct.**Article 243K(1) states that the**superintendence, direction and control of the preparation of **electoral rolls**for Panchayat elections are vested in the SEC. This includes revising, updating, or correcting electoral rolls for local body polls.**

Statement II is incorrect.**Elections to cooperative societies are**not**conducted by the SEC. As per the Constitution of India, (97th Amendment) Act, 2011,**Article 243ZK(2) ,**the superintendence, direction and control of the preparation of electoral rolls for, and the conduct of, all elections of a co-operative society in the state, shall vest in such**an **authority or body, as may be provided by the legislature of a State. **

Statement III is incorrect.**Under**Article 243F(2) **of the Constitution of India, if there is a dispute about a panchayat member's disqualification, it must be decided by**the authority and process specified by state law**.

statementLines: Which of the following were the recommendations of the Gadgil Committee (1988) on Panchayati Raj institutions?
I. Providing a fixed five-year term for Panchayati Raj Institutions (PRIs).
II. Direct elections to Panchayats at all three levels viz, village, intermediate, and district.
III. Establishing a State Finance Commission to allocate finances to Panchayats.
IV. Reserving seats for Schedule Castes, Schedule Tribes, and women in proportion to their population.
Which of the statements given above are correct?
questions.questionText: Which of the following were the recommendations of the Gadgil Committee (1988) on Panchayati Raj institutions? I. Providing a fixed five-year term for Panchayati Raj Institutions (PRIs). II. Direct elections to Panchayats at all three levels viz, village, intermediate, and district. III. Establishing a State Finance Commission to allocate finances to Panchayats. IV. Reserving seats for Schedule Castes, Schedule Tribes, and women in proportion to their population. Which of the statements given above are correct?
questions.explanationMarkdown:
**Explanation:** Option d is the correct answer.**The**Gadgil Committee (1988) **officially the Committee on Policy and Programmes was set up to examine how Panchayati Raj Institutions (PRIs) could be made effective and durable.**

Statement I is correct.**To strengthen continuity and regular functioning of local governance, the**Gadgil**committee proposed a**fixed five-year tenure**for PRIs at all three tiers, thereby enhancing democratic participation and accountability.**

Statement II is correct.**The**Gadgil **committee proposed**direct elections at all three tiers**of the Panchayati Raj system that is at the village, intermediate and district level.**

Statement III is correct.**To enable effective functioning, the committee called for financial autonomy, empowering PRIs to levy, collect, and appropriate taxes and duties. Additionally, it proposed the establishment of a**State Finance **Commission **for fair financial allocation.**

Statement IV is correct.**The**Gadgil**committee stressed the need for equitable representation by proposing**proportional reservations for Scheduled Castes, Scheduled Tribes, and women**based on their**population share**.

statementLines: Which of the following statements is/are correct regarding the Digital Personal Data Protection Act, 2023 (DPDP Act)?
I. The Act applies to the processing of digital personal data only within the territory of India.
II. It broadens the scope for denying information under the Right to Information Act.
III. It allows for the imposition of monetary penalties for the breach of its provisions.
Select the correct answer using the code given below:
questions.questionText: Which of the following statements is/are correct regarding the Digital Personal Data Protection Act, 2023 (DPDP Act)? I. The Act applies to the processing of digital personal data only within the territory of India. II. It broadens the scope for denying information under the Right to Information Act. III. It allows for the imposition of monetary penalties for the breach of its provisions. Select the correct answer using the code given below:
questions.explanationMarkdown:
**Explanation:** Option a is the correct answer. **The Digital Personal Data Protection Act, 2023 (DPDP Act) is intended to "provide for the processing of digital personal data in a manner that r**ecognises both the right of individuals to protect their personal data **and the need to process such personal data for lawful purposes."**

Statement I is incorrect. **The Act applies to the processing of digital personal data within the territory of India where the personal data is collected in digital form; or in non-digital form and digitised subsequently.**The Act also applies to **processing of digital personal data outside the territory of India,**if such processing is in connection with any activity related to offering of goods or services to Data Principals within the territory of India.**

Statement II is correct. Section 44(3) of the DPDP Act mentions a**change that will be made**in Section 8(1)(j) of the **RTI Act. **Section 44(3) of the DPDP Act shortens the clause in the RTI Act to** broaden the scope for denying information.**It says: "In section 8 of the Right to Information Act, 2005, in sub-section (1), for clause (j), the following clause shall be substituted, namely: - "(j) information which relates to personal information" This change, RTI activists say, will greatly reduce the amount of information that government agencies are obligated to disclose under the 2005 Act. Section 8(1)(j) of RTI Act provided that: "There shall be no obligation to give any citizen, information which relates to personal information the disclosure of which has no relationship to any public activity or interest, or which would cause unwarranted invasion of the privacy of the individual unless the Central Public Information Officer or the State Public Information Officer or the appellate authority is satisfied that the larger public interest justifies the disclosure of such information."**

Statement III is correct. **The Act provides for the establishment of the Data Protection Board of India. Section 33 of the Act says that If the Board determines on conclusion of an inquiry**that breach of the provisions of **this Act by a person is significant, it may impose such monetary penalty specified in the Schedule. **

statementLines: Which of the following statements with regard to the areas under the Panchayats (Extension to the Scheduled Areas) Act, 1996 are correct?
I. The Gram Sabha in such areas is empowered to exercise control over money lending to Scheduled Tribes.
II. The recommendations of the Gram Sabha or the Panchayats at the appropriate level is mandatory prior to grant of mining lease for minor minerals in the Scheduled Areas.
III. At least one-half of the total number of seats in every Panchayat in Scheduled Areas shall be reserved for the Scheduled Tribes.
IV. Every Gram Sabha shall approve the plans for social and economic development before such plans are taken up for implementation by the Panchayat at the village level.
Select the correct answer using the code given below:
questions.questionText: Which of the following statements with regard to the areas under the Panchayats (Extension to the Scheduled Areas) Act, 1996 are correct? I. The Gram Sabha in such areas is empowered to exercise control over money lending to Scheduled Tribes. II. The recommendations of the Gram Sabha or the Panchayats at the appropriate level is mandatory prior to grant of mining lease for minor minerals in the Scheduled Areas. III. At least one-half of the total number of seats in every Panchayat in Scheduled Areas shall be reserved for the Scheduled Tribes. IV. Every Gram Sabha shall approve the plans for social and economic development before such plans are taken up for implementation by the Panchayat at the village level. Select the correct answer using the code given below:
questions.explanationMarkdown:
**Explanation:** Option d is the correct answer**.**The Panchayats (Extension to the Scheduled Areas) Act, 1996 (PESA)**ensures self-governance**through Gram Sabhas for people living in Scheduled Areas. It mandates consultation, control, and planning powers to tribal communities over their resources and development.**

Statement I is correct. **Under**Section**4(m)(v) of**PESA, **the Gram Sabha has the authority to control money-lending activities to Scheduled Tribes in Scheduled Areas**. This provision helps safeguard tribal communities from exploitative lending practices.
**Statement II is correct.**Under **Section**4(k), t**he PESA Act makes the**prior recommendation of the Gram Sabha **mandatory**for granting mining leases for minor minerals. This provision is aimed at ensuring informed consent and protection of tribal interests.**

Statement III is correct. As per Section**4(g) of PESA, t**he reservation for Scheduled Tribes in every Panchayat in the Scheduled Areas **shall be not less than one-half of the total number of seats**. Additionally, all Chairperson posts at every level in such Panchayats shall be reserved for Scheduled Tribes.

**Statement IV is correct.**According to **Section**4(e)(i)**, the Gram Sabha must**approve plans, programmes and projects **for social and economic development**before the Panchayat implements them at the village level. Also, under**Section**4(e)(ii)**, Gram Sabha is responsible for identifying beneficiaries under poverty alleviation and other programmes..

statementLines: With reference to the Constitution of India, consider the following statements with regard to a District Planning Committee (DPC):
I. At least two-thirds of its members are elected by and from amongst the elected members of Panchayats and Municipalities in the concerned district based on rural-urban population ratio.
II. It must include representatives of the Government of India and the respective State government to ensure alignment with national and state level planning priorities.
Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
questions.questionText: With reference to the Constitution of India, consider the following statements with regard to a District Planning Committee (DPC): I. At least two-thirds of its members are elected by and from amongst the elected members of Panchayats and Municipalities in the concerned district based on rural-urban population ratio. II. It must include representatives of the Government of India and the respective State government to ensure alignment with national and state level planning priorities. Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
questions.explanationMarkdown:
**Explanation:** Option d is the correct answer**. The**District Planning Committee (DPC)**is a constitutional body established under**Article 243ZD**of the Indian Constitution. It aims to consolidate the developmental plans prepared by Panchayats and Municipalities within a district into a single, cohesive district-level plan.**

Statement I is incorrect. **As per Article 243ZD(2),**not less than four-fifths**of the total members of a DPC must be**elected by and from amongst**the elected members of the Panchayats and the Municipalities in the district. This must be done**in proportion to the rural and urban population**of the district.**

Statement II is incorrect. **There is**no constitutional requirement** to include representatives of the Central Government in a District Planning Committee. While Metropolitan Planning Committees (under Article 243ZE) do involve Central and State Government representatives, the DPC does not.

questions.explanationMarkdown: **Explanation:** Option c is the correct answer.**The PESA Act was enacted to extend the provisions of 73rd Constitutional Amendment Act to the Fifth Schedule areas. The provisions of this Act address some of the most complex problems of exploitation and marginalization of the tribals. The act**did not have any provisions of creating any autonomous regions. **

questions.explanationMarkdown: **Explanation:** Option b is the correct answer**. As per Article 243R(2), The Legislature of a State may, by law, provide - (a) for the representation in a Municipality of - (i) persons having special knowledge or experience in Municipal administration;**(there is no mention of the persons **having special knowledge or experience in science, arts or public service, so option I is incorrect) **(ii)** the members of the House of the People **and the members of the Legislative Assembly of the State**representing **constituencies which comprise wholly or partly the Municipal area; ( option II is correct) **(iii)** the members of the Council of States**and the members of the Legislative Council of the State**registered as **electors within the Municipal area; ( option III is correct) ** (iv) the Chairpersons of the Committees constituted under clause (5) of article 243S (any other committees than the Ward Committees) Provided that the persons referred to in paragraph (i) (i.e.persons having special knowledge or experience in Municipal administration) shall not have the right to vote in the meetings of the Municipality.

questions.questionText: With reference to the local self governance in India, consider the following statements about the Notified Area Committees in a state: I. It is a statutory body established by an Act of the concerned state government. II. All the members of the committee are nominated by the concerned state government. Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
questions.explanationMarkdown:
**Explanation:** Option b is the correct answer**. A Notified Area Committee (NAC) is created for regions that are rapidly urbanising, such as industrial townships. These areas may not yet qualify for full-fledged municipal status but require special administrative arrangements.**

Statement I is incorrect. **A NAC is**not a statutory body**. It is formed** through **a**notification by the state government **and not through a separate law/Act, **which is why it is termed "notified." However, its functioning is guided by the provisions of the**State Municipal Act**.**

Statement II is correct. **The NAC is an**entirely nominated body**. All its members, including the chairman, are**appointed by the state government by nomination**. There is**no provision for direct or indirect elections**in its composition.

statementLines: With reference to urban local governments in India, consider the following:
I. Town Area Committees
II. Cantonment Boards
III. Townships
How many of the above are established under a law passed by the Parliament?
questions.questionText: With reference to urban local governments in India, consider the following: I. Town Area Committees II. Cantonment Boards III. Townships How many of the above are established under a law passed by the Parliament?
questions.explanationMarkdown: **Explanation:** Option a is the correct answer**. In India, urban governance is carried out by a mix of elected municipalities and other bodies created for specific administrative or functional purposes. While municipalities derive their legal basis from the 74th Constitutional Amendment, there are other bodies that operate under laws passed by the**Parliament**.** Option I is incorrect**. A Town Area Committee is an Urban Local Body (ULB) established to manage the administration of a small town.** It is created through a specific Act passed by the State Legislature, which also governs its **composition, functions, and other related matters.**The committee may be entirely elected, entirely nominated by the State government, or a combination of both. As a semi-municipal authority, it is responsible for a limited range of civic functions, including drainage, roads, street lighting, and waste management.** Option II is correct**. Cantonment Boards are a unique form of urban governance, functioning in areas where military establishments are located. These are created under the**Cantonments Act, 2006**, a comprehensive legislation passed by the**Parliament of India**. These boards are statutory urban local bodies, but they are**not covered under the 74th Constitutional Amendment**. They are administered by the**Ministry of Defence**and comprise both nominated and elected members. According to Sections 12-15 of the Cantonments Act, the commanding officer of the military station acts as the president of the Board. Some examples are the**Pune Cantonment Board**and the**Secunderabad Cantonment Board**.** Option III is incorrect**. Townships are planned urban settlements typically developed by public sector enterprises or private companies to house their employees. These include well-known examples like the**Jamshedpur Township**(developed by Tata Steel),**BHEL Township in Haridwar**, and**ONGC townships**in various parts of India. These townships are**not created by any law passed by the Parliament or State Legislature**. Instead, they are administered by the corporate entity itself or through informal arrangements. They do not have elected councils, nor do they fall under the purview of state municipal acts or central legislation.

statementLines: With reference to the Constitution (Seventy-Fourth Amendment) Act, 1992, consider the following provisions:
I. Conduct regular elections to municipalities every five years.
II. Not less than one-third of the total number of seats to be filled by direct election in every Municipality shall be reserved for women
III. Creation of Metropolitan Planning Committees in every Metropolitan area.

IV. Constituting the Ward Committees in territory of a municipality with a population of 3 lakhs or more. As per the Constitution of India, which of the above provisions is/are mandatory to be followed by the State governments regarding the administration of local bodies?
questions.questionText: With reference to the Constitution (Seventy-Fourth Amendment) Act, 1992, consider the following provisions: I. Conduct regular elections to municipalities every five years. II. Not less than one-third of the total number of seats to be filled by direct election in every Municipality shall be reserved for women III. Creation of Metropolitan Planning Committees in every Metropolitan area. IV. Constituting the Ward Committees in territory of a municipality with a population of 3 lakhs or more. As per the Constitution of India, which of the above provisions is/are mandatory to be followed by the State governments regarding the administration of local bodies?
questions.explanationMarkdown:
**Explanation:** Option d is the correct answer**. The**74th Constitutional Amendment Act, 1992**provided a constitutional status to**Urban Local Bodies (ULBs)**by adding**Part IXA (Articles 243P to 243ZG)**, aiming to**strengthen democratic governance at the grassroots**in urban areas.**

Statement I is correct**.**Article 243U(1)**mandates that a municipality shall have a**fixed term of 5 years**from its first meeting. Article** 243U(3)**further requires that elections must be held**before the expiry**of the municipality's duration or within**six months**if it is dissolved earlier.**Thus, the conduct of regular elections every five years is a mandatory constitutional requirement. **

Statement II is correct**.**Article 243T(3)**explicitly states that**not less than one-third**of the total number of seats (including those reserved for SC/STs) must be reserved for**women**.**Therefore, the reservation of one-third seats for **women is a mandatory provision under the Constitution.**

Statement III is correct**.**Article 243ZE(1)**of the Constitution clearly provides that**a Metropolitan Planning **Committee (MPC) must be constituted in every Metropolitan area. **A**metropolitan area**is defined in**Article 243Pc)**as one having a population of**ten lakhs (1 million) or more**.**Hence, the creation of such committees is a **constitutionally mandated requirement.**

Statement IV is correct**.**Article 243S(1)**of the Constitution mandates that -**There shall be constituted Wards **Committees, consisting of one or more wards, within the territorial area of a Municipality having a population of **three lakhs or more**. While the**details regarding composition and territorial jurisdiction are to be determined by **the State Legislature**under**Article 243S(2)**, the**establishment of such committees is a constitutional requirement**and not left to the discretion of the states.

statementLines: Triple Test, as laid down by the Supreme Court and often seen in the news, relates to which of the following? educational institutions.
questions.questionText: Triple Test, as laid down by the Supreme Court and often seen in the news, relates to which of the following? educational institutions.

options.a: Criteria to identify the creamy layer among Other Backward Classes (OBCs) for central government jobs.
options.b: Conditions for providing reservations to Other Backward Classes (OBCs) in local body elections.
options.c: Legal validity of extending provisions of reservations for Economically Weaker Sections to private unaided
options.d: Parameters to be used while conducting a Caste Census.

questions.explanationMarkdown: **Explanation:** Option b is the correct answer**. In recent years, the issue of OBC reservations in local body elections has become a focal point of judicial scrutiny. The Supreme Court of India, through multiple judgments including the landmark Vikas Kishanrao Gawali vs State of Maharashtra (2021), has established a constitutional framework popularly known as the**Triple Test**. The Triple Test, as prescribed by the Supreme Court, lays down three essential conditions that must be fulfilled by a State before providing reservation for OBCs in local bodies:**1. Constitution of a dedicated commission**to examine the backwardness of OBCs in local governance.**2. Empirical collection of data**by this commission on the nature and extent of the backwardness and specify the reservation proportion based on the commission's findings**3. Capping the total reservation**(including SCs, STs, and OBCs) at**50%**of the total seats, in line with the ceiling established by earlier constitutional benches. The Supreme Court reiterated this framework in cases involving Maharashtra, Tamil Nadu, and most recently in Telangana, where it struck down excessive OBC reservations that breached the 50% ceiling without following the triple test.

**Explanation:** Option a is the correct answer**. The Fifth Schedule of the Indian Constitution outlines special provisions for the administration and governance of Scheduled Areas and Scheduled Tribes in certain states. Statement I is correct**. Statement II is incorrect**. It is**not the President**, but the**Governor of the state**who has the authority to**exclude or **modify the application of any Parliamentary or State law** to a Scheduled Area.

statementLines: With reference to the Tribes Advisory Council (TAC) established in certain states of India, consider the following statements:

II. The Council consists of a maximum of 20 members.
III. The Governor nominates all members of the Tribal Advisory Council.

questions.questionText: With reference to the Tribes Advisory Council (TAC) established in certain states of India, consider the following statements: I. It is established under the provisions of the Sixth Schedule of the Constitution of India. II. The Council consists of a maximum of 20 members. III. The Governor nominates all members of the Tribal Advisory Council. Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
questions.explanationMarkdown:
**Explanation:** Option a is the correct answer. **

Statement I is incorrect: **The Tribal Advisory Council (TAC) is**not created under the Sixth Schedule**. It is created under the**Fifth Schedule**, which deals with the governance and welfare of tribal populations in**States having **Scheduled Areas**. In States without Scheduled Areas but with significant tribal populations, the**President may direct**the creation of a similar council.**

Statement II is correct: **Under**Paragraph 4(1) of the Fifth Schedule**of the Constitution, a Tribal Advisory Council (TAC) shall consist of**not more than 20 members**. The body exists primarily to**advise the State Government**on matters related to the welfare and advancement of Scheduled Tribes.**

Statement III is incorrect: **The Governor does**not**nominate all members.**Paragraph 4(1) of the Fifth Schedule**specifies that**three-fourths**of TAC members must be**elected Scheduled Tribe MLAs** of the State Legislative Assembly. Provided that if the number of representatives of the Scheduled Tribes in the Legislative Assembly of the State is less than the number of seats in the Tribes Advisory Council to be filled by such representatives, the remaining seats shall be filled by other members of those tribes.

statementLines: With reference to the Sixth Schedule of the Constitution of India, consider the following statements:
I. It was not part of the original Constitution and was added later through a Constitutional Amendment Act.
II. The provisions of the Sixth Schedule are currently applicable to the administration of Tribal Areas in Arunachal Pradesh, Tripura, Meghalaya, and Manipur.
III. The tribal regions covered by the Sixth Schedule are completely exempt from the executive authority of the respective States.
Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
questions.questionText: With reference to the Sixth Schedule of the Constitution of India, consider the following statements: I. It was not part of the original Constitution and was added later through a Constitutional Amendment Act. II. The provisions of the Sixth Schedule are currently applicable to the administration of Tribal Areas in Arunachal Pradesh, Tripura, Meghalaya, and Manipur. III. The tribal regions covered by the Sixth Schedule are completely exempt from the executive authority of the respective States. Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
questions.explanationMarkdown:
**Explanation:** Option d is the correct answer. **The Sixth Schedule provides**autonomous governance for tribal areas in Assam, Meghalaya, Tripura, and Mizoram.**It establishes Autonomous District and Regional Councils with legislative, executive, judicial, and financial powers over local matters. 

Statement I is incorrect: **The Sixth Schedule**was part of the original Constitution (1950)**. It was drafted in the Constituent Assembly on the recommendation of a report of a committee led by Gopinath Bordoloi to protect tribal customs while ensuring administrative flexibility in the Northeast. It was**not**added through any later amendments.**

Statement II is incorrect: Under Article 242(2), **the Sixth Schedule applies only to**four states**:**Assam, Meghalaya, **Tripura, and Mizoram**.**Manipur is not covered**under the Sixth Schedule.**

Statement III is incorrect: **Tribal areas under the Sixth Schedule**are not completely outside**the State's executive authority. They enjoy a degree of autonomy through District and Regional Councils, but they**remain under the **executive jurisdiction of the respective State Governments**.

questions.questionText: With reference to the powers of District Councils created in the autonomous district under the Sixth Schedule of the Constitution of India, consider the following statements: I. They can establish Village Courts for resolving disputes among tribal members within the district. II. They can legislate on matters like marriage and divorce for tribal communities within the district. III. They can impose taxes on activities such as the entry of goods into the market and their sale within the district. Which of the statements given above are correct?
questions.explanationMarkdown:
**Explanation:** Option a is the correct answer. **District Councils under the Sixth Schedule administer**Autonomous Districts**through a**30-member body**(26 elected, 4 nominated by the Governor). They exercise**law-making**,**administrative**, and**local governance**powers. The**Governor can reorganise autonomous districts**, but these areas remain under the**executive authority of the State **Government**.**

Statement I is correct: **Under**Paragraph 4(1)**of the Sixth Schedule, District Councils may establish**Village Courts**to try civil and criminal cases involving Scheduled Tribe members.**Paragraph 4(2)**provides for**District and Regional **Council Courts as appellate bodies**.**

Statement II is correct: **District Councils have law-making authority under**Paragraph 3(1)**over matters such as**inheritance, marriage, divorce, social customs, land, village administration, and forests (excluding reserved **forests)**. However,**Paragraph 3(3)**requires that such laws**take effect only after the Governor's assent**, ensuring constitutional oversight.**

Statement III is correct: **Financial powers are provided under**Paragraph 8**, enabling District Councils to**levy and **collect taxes** such as land revenue, taxes on professions and trades, and taxes on entry of goods into markets.

statementLines: Consider the following statements:
1. Aadhaar metadata cannot be stored for more than three months.
2. The state cannot enter into any contract with private corporations for sharing of Aadhaar data.
3. Aadhaar is mandatory for obtaining insurance products.
4. Aadhaar is mandatory for getting benefits funded out of the Consolidated Fund of India.
Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
questions.questionText: Consider the following statements: 1. Aadhaar metadata cannot be stored for more than three months. 2. The state cannot enter into any contract with private corporations for sharing of Aadhaar data. 3. Aadhaar is mandatory for obtaining insurance products. 4. Aadhaar is mandatory for getting benefits funded out of the Consolidated Fund of India. Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
questions.explanationMarkdown:
**Explanation:** Option b is the correct answer.**

Statement 1 is incorrect**. A Constitution Bench of the Supreme Court ruled that the Aadhaar metadata cannot be stored beyond six months. The Aadhar Act had allowed storage of such data for five years.**

Statement 2 is correct.**The Supreme Court has struck down Section 57 that allowed sharing of data with private entities. But has allowed the unique number to be used for government schemes and subsidies.**

Statement 3 is incorrect.**Aadhaar is not mandatory for obtaining insurance products. Insurance regulator IRDAI has advised insurers not to mandatorily seek Aadhaar and PAN/Form 60 from customers, existing or new, for KYC (Know Your Customer) purpose. The insurers can accept Aadhaar as one of the documents for KYC, only when the same is offered voluntarily by the proposer/policyholder.**

Statement 4 is correct**. The amendment made to Section 7 of the Aadhaar (Targeted delivery of financial and other subsidies, benefits and services) Act 2016, make Aadhar mandatory for beneficiaries for receipt of a subsidy, benefit or service for which the expenditure is incurred from the consolidated fund.
